name: vdk-control-api-auth

metadata.name: vdk-control-api-auth
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Versatile Data Kit plugin library provides support for authentication.

documentation: # vdk-control-api-auth vdk-control-api-auth is a plugin library that implements authentication utilities used by vdk-control-cli and other plugins. ## Usage This is a library plugin, not a runnable plugin, and it is intended to be used as a dependency for other plugins, which need to authenticate users. To use the library within a plugin or another Versatile Data Kit component, just import the `Authentication` class, and create an instance of it. The different authentication flows require different parameters to be specified. Once everything is done, in order to authenticate, call `.authenticate()` on the `Authentication` instance. Example Usage: ```python from vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.authentication import Authentication auth = Authentication( token="", authorization_url="https://some-authorization-endpoint", auth_type="api-token", ) auth.authenticate() # authenticate auth.read_access_token() # fetch the cached access token ``` ## Build and testing In order to build and test a plugin go to the plugin directory and use `../build-plugin.sh` script to build it

modules.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_exception
documentation: Copyright 2023-2024 Broadcom
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_exception.VDKAuthException
documentation:
The VDKAuthException is custom exception type following the coding standard
for error handling: see the project contributing documentation

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_exception.VDKInvalidAuthParamError
documentation:
The VDKInvalidAuthParamError is a custom exception type derived from
the base VDKAuthException type. It is raised when a parameter needed
for authentication is missing/not provided or otherwise invalid.

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_exception.VDKLoginFailedError
documentation:
The VDKLoginFailedError is a custom exception type derived from the
base VDKAuthException type. It is raised when an error occurs while
going through the actual authentication flow, i.e., when something
happens while connecting to a third party endpoint.

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_exception.VDKAuthOSError
documentation:
The VDKAuthOSError is a custom exception type derived from the base
VDKAuthException type. It is raised when an error in the underlying
Operating System arises.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.AuthConfig.api_token_authorization_url
documentation:
Location of the API Token OAuth2 provider. Same as login --api-token-authorization-server-url
This is used as default.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.AuthConfig.api_token
documentation:
API Token for the OAuth2 provider used in exchange for Access Token
Same as login --api-token.
This is used as default.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.AuthConfig.local_config_folder
documentation:
Keeps local configuration of Control CLI like cached tokens, authorization uris, etc.

Solves following use-cases:

One scenario where two different users would like to be able to log in on the same machine.
People sometimes share a development environment as well. Concrete example is two different user
using the same Jenkins machine

Also, another use-case is if you want to connect to two different Control Services with different authentication.
For example, during development, a Demo Control Service works with staging provider but in the
deployment in production works with Production. So in order to switch you need to re-login every time.
But now you can just switch tabs (with different environment variables set)
:return:

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.CredentialsCache.save_credentials
documentation:
Save the credentials data securely
\:param content: the content of the credentials. It's a text based format.
\:return:

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.CredentialsCache.delete_credentials
documentation: Delete any credentials data stored.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.CredentialsCache.read_credentials
documentation:
Read the stored credentials data
\:return: the data

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.InMemoryCredentialsCache
documentation:
A class providing the equivalent of an in-memory LocalFolderCredentialsCache.
This configuration is available as long as the python process is running,
and is lost when the process is terminated.

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.SingletonInMemoryCredentialsCache
documentation:
Singleton class for InMemAuthConfiguration,
ensuring that there is only one instance of
InMemAuthConfiguration with shared state across the application.

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.auth_config.LocalFolderCredentialsCache
documentation:
A class responsible for managing the configuration files in the vdk configuration
folder

modules.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth

SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.AuthenticationCache

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.AuthenticationCache.__init__
documentation:
---
param access_token: |-
  Cached access token used during requests;
  See <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-1.4>
param access_token_expiration_time: |-
  When the access token expires
  (epoch seconds -- same as time.time() + "expires_in" value returned
  by the authorization server that generates the access token.)
param api_token: |-
  Token used to grant access to the API,
  including exchange for access token.
param api_token_authorization_url: The URL which exchanges api_token for access token.
param authorization_url: Token or Authorization URI used to exchange grant for access
  token
param client_id: |-
  The client identifier;
  See <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1>
param refresh_token: |-
  Used in refresh token grant type flow;
  See <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-1.5>
---

Class used to keep the fields in the vdk-cred.json file from which
authorization and authentication tokens can be taken. Used only when
caching is enabled.

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.AuthenticationCacheSerDe
documentation: Class used for cache serialization and deserialization.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.BaseAuth.read_access_token
documentation:
Read access token from cache or fetch it from Authorization server.
If not available in cache it will get it using provided configuration during VDK CLI login to fetch it.
If it detects that token is about to expire it will try to refresh it.
\:return: the access token or None if it cannot detect any credentials.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.BaseAuth.read_cached_access_token_only
documentation:
Read access token from credentials cache and will return it if available.
Will not check if it is valid (for example not expired) but return it directly.
Will not try to fetch it form Authorization provider if it is not available. Instead, will simply return None

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.BaseAuth.acquire_and_cache_access_token
documentation: Acquires and caches access token

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.BaseAuth.update_api_token_authorization_url
documentation: Updates and caches authorization URL

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.BaseAuth.update_api_token

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.BaseAuth.update_refresh_token

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.base_auth.BaseAuth.update_access_token

modules.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.authentication
documentation: Copyright 2023-2024 Broadcom
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.authentication.Authentication

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.authentication.Authentication.__init__
documentation:
---
param auth_discovery_url: Token or Authorization URI used to exchange grant for access
  token.
param auth_type: |-
  What type of authentication should be used (e.g., refresh_token,
  basic_auth, etc.).
param authorization_url: The URL which exchanges token for access token.
param cache_locally: |-
  A flag, indicating if credentials should be cached locally (in a
  file).
  Deprecated: use credentials_cache instead to pass how credentials are stored explicitly.
param client_id: |-
  The client identifier;
  See <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1>
param client_secret: |-
  The client identifier;
  See <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1>
param credentials_cache: The configuration store used to actually store the credentials
param password: A user's password in case basic authentication is used.
param possible_jwt_user_keys: |-
  Used by get_authenticated_username to try to discover correct username if OAuth2 and JWT token is used. It is a list of keys where the first existing key in a JTW token is returned.
  Defaults to some common user keys.
param token: |-
  OAuth api token or a refresh token as specified in
  <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-1.5> and used
  to obtain access token from the authorization server.
param username: A user's username in case basic authentication is used.
---

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.authentication.Authentication.get_authenticated_username
documentation:
Extract user name about currently authenticated user.
It tries to get username or if not available user email or if that fails some user id.
The reponse format should not be relied upon, it's meant to be used for logging and telemetry.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.authentication.Authentication.read_access_token
documentation: Read access token from cache.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.authentication.Authentication.__authenticate_with_api_token
documentation: Authenticate by providing only a OAuth2 API token.

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.authentication.Authentication.__authenticate_with_authorization_code
documentation:
Authenticate with authorization code as described in
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-1.3.1
This type of authentication relies on pkce
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636
:return:
NOTE: Implementation to be added with subsequent PR
Main class used for authentication.

modules.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.autorization_code_auth
documentation: Copyright 2023-2024 Broadcom
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

functions.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.autorization_code_auth.generate_pkce_codes
documentation:
Generate PKCE code challenge and code verifier necessary during Authorization Code Workflow
as described in RFC 7636 (see <https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7636>)
\:return: code_verifier, code_challenge, code_challenge_method

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.autorization_code_auth.LoginHandler

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.autorization_code_auth.LoginHandler.__init__
documentation:
---
param auth: A BaseAuth object.
param client_id: |-
  The client identifier;
  See <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1>
param client_secret: |-
  The client identifier;
  See <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1>
param code_verifier: |-
  A string used to correlate the authorization request to the token
  request as described in <https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7636>
param exchange_endpoint: The URL which exchanges token for access token.
param redirect_uri: |-
  URL for the authorization server to redirect back to (as defined
  in <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-3.1.2>).
---

class_variables.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.autorization_code_auth.LoginHandler.REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY
documentation: nosec

Class to handle the updates of the authentication parameters and the log
in process itself.

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.autorization_code_auth.MyHttpRequestHandler
documentation:
Class used by RedirectAuthentication to handle the GET redirect request in order to acquire the refresh and access
tokens. In essence the class creates Authentication object which fills the necessary configuration fields for the
credentials authentication type.

classes.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.autorization_code_auth.RedirectAuthentication

methods.name: vdk.plugin.control_api_auth.autorization_code_auth.RedirectAuthentication.__init__
documentation:
---
param auth: A BaseAuth object.
param client_id: The client identifier of the OAuth2 Application. Find out more <https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1>
param client_secret: The client secret of the OAuth2 Application. Find out more <https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1>
param oauth2_discovery_url: Token or Authorization URI used to exchange grant for
  access token
param oauth2_exchange_url: The URL which exchanges token for access token.
param redirect_uri: |-
  The redirect uri which will be used in Authorization Workflow.
  Per <https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8252#section-7.3> it should be <http://127.0.0.1>
  so there should not be reason to override the default except for tests
param redirect_uri_default_port: |-
  The default port to use for redirect uri unless env. variable 'OAUTH2_REDIRECT_URI_PORT' is used.
  If None - then random one is assigned
---

Class used to start web browser and http process which will handle single GET request and shut down.
The browser will redirect to the http process which handles the request.

Making the port which we use for authentication constant enable integration with some OAuth2 authorization server
(e.g VMware Cloud) which require a redirect uri when creating OAuth2 app which needs to be set to the exact
value which is used in the redirect url. Before we took random port from the ones available which prevented us
from configuring the URI in the application options.
It might be good idea for this to be configurable, so we can switch to random ports when necessary
